reason: Enabling FIPS mode requires adding the fips=1 kernel boot parameter, which modifies the bootloader configuration and forces the system to operate in FIPS mode. This change is not supported on an already installed system and can render the system unbootable or cause severe compliance and functionality issues. Therefore it is considered too dangerous to remediate without a full reinstall.
estimated_impact: reboot required; bootloader modification; FIPS mode activation; potential system unbootable; non‑supported change on existing installation
